title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
Harakat, who calls himself Ra-Harakht, leads the investigators into a roofless sanctuary containing star charts, a statue of Horus, and various ritual objects. Professor Yadley reveals to Ellery that Harakat is actually a former world-renowned Egyptologist named Striker. Yadley explains that Striker became mentally deranged following severe dehydration during an excavation in the Valley of the Kings, causing him to muddle various Egyptian religious symbols and rituals together.

During the investigation, Officer Vane questions Striker regarding his departure from West Virginia. Striker claims he left five months ago following a "Cooper's Hill ceremony," which Yadley clarifies is a reference to an ancient Egyptian sunset ritual involving a specific type of cake. When Vane asks about a man named Croscott, Striker claims Croscott fled the sanctuary. Paul Roman, who met Striker in Pittsburgh in February, dismisses the religious aspects of the movement as a way to lure "rubes," admitting his true interest is promoting nudism for health benefits. Striker repeatedly claims that the arrival on Oyster Island was "all arranged," though he refuses to specify by whom.

The questioning reveals that twenty-three nudists live on the island, lured by advertisements and Roman’s proselytizing. These individuals are transported from a local train station to a dock on Dr. Temple's property and then ferried across by an old man named Ketchum. Officer Vane gathers the terrified converts, preventing them from leaving the island while he verifies their identities. As the investigators prepare to depart, Dr. Temple urges Hester Lincoln to leave the group of "frauds, thieves, and idiots," but Hester remains standing beside Roman.
